h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: sketch's a bit murky on that side, but the arrow's got to come into heron, because heron's the bigger silhouette on the page.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: ...no, half that read is the smear doing the work, that's me filling in from what I want it to be.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange seemed headed toward naming the source species and confirming a food-source-to-heron arrow.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Mark it "source unclear → heron" before it ships — rather flag it now than have the modelers running a phantom periwinkle line by next quarter.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner applies a new evidence standard by choosing uncertainty tagging over an overconfident species claim.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Notation stays food source → eater; size is off the key. Eater side is settled: heron. Tail label first: read the exposed source, then mark source → heron. If the photo won’t say, tag the source as unclear before it goes upstream.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After the learner persists in size-based inference despite murkiness, the tutor shifts from arrow-direction checking to an evidence-standard mechanism for readable versus unclear source labels.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Eater side is settled: heron. Tail label first: read the exposed source, then mark source → heron. If the photo won’t say, tag the source as unclear before it goes upstream.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is precise, economical, and fitted to the blockage: it separates known eater from uncertain source and gives the learner a usable criterion for when to flag uncertainty.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Food source → eater stays. This tail is not readable enough for a species claim, so the checklist line is `source unclear → heron` with the uncertainty flag. No phantom periwinkle goes upstream; send it that way, then back to rounds.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor takes up the learner's revised uncertainty frame and converts it into the final checklist action rather than merely praising the insight.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor takes up the learner's revised uncertainty frame and converts it into the final checklist action rather than merely praising the insight.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The mechanism is precise, economical, and fitted to the blockage: it separates known eater from uncertain source and gives the learner a usable criterion for when to flag uncertainty.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I just want to be sure before writing the same number down three times.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Right — four old squares fit inside, so the area box takes four, not two.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange is moving toward computing each empty box by following the tutor's guided counts.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Two and two and two and two around the small one — eight. Four and four and four and four around the big one — sixteen. [sets the two numbers down beside the still-empty box] Eight and sixteen, side by side, before anything goes in.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the new perimeter comparison procedure before filling the label, showing a changed evidence standard in action.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Here is the old 2-by-2 area, copied across and down. Count those old squares inside the new one before the pencil goes in the area box.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's area confusion with a concrete representation, but does not explicitly take stock of a failed route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Here is the old 2-by-2 area, copied across and down. Count those old squares inside the new one before the pencil goes in the area box.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The block-counting device is precise, usable, and fitted to the learner's question about why doubled side length yields area four.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn after the L4 pivot.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I just want to be sure before writing the same number down three times.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to keep pressing whether board distinctions can affect the buyer's actual situation at the counter.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Buyers, then. Pressure by many people — which many people? Nobody walked home from work tonight knowing each other's names; there's no queue forming on a Wednesday over a small loaf.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner uses the tutor's lever-classification device and immediately tests the selected category against a concrete social condition.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
It does not lower the price tonight. That has to be said.

[The tutor writes across both columns: “A DISTINCTION DOES NOT PAY THE BILL. IT CHANGES THE DEMAND.” Traffic cuts through the open window. The chalk is set beside the pinned price list.]

Take the chalk. Two lines only.

[On the board:]

“CAN SAY FROM THIS PAPER:”

“MUST ASK BEFORE BLAME LANDS:”
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After the learner pressures the insufficiency of the clean evidence distinction, the tutor openly concedes that limit and changes the task into a public demand-forming device.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
Take the chalk. Two lines only.

[On the board:]

“CAN SAY FROM THIS PAPER:”

“MUST ASK BEFORE BLAME LANDS:”
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is economical and usable because it separates public evidence from required missing evidence, though it still depends on later work to solve obligation and collective leverage.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no recognitive learner pivot, so there is no later tutor uptake of a revised learner self-framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Buyers, then. Pressure by many people — which many people? Nobody walked home from work tonight knowing each other's names; there's no queue forming on a Wednesday over a small loaf.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no recognitive learner pivot, so there is no later tutor uptake of a revised learner self-framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Sheet's done, every line, I checked it — three times, I think.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: it's not the same number though, is it. Three times static. That's not her knee.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange seemed headed toward getting the completed sheet signed quickly despite the waiting person outside.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Can I put a bag on the front lip, off centre, see if a leg lifts? Not heavy, only — I want to see if it goes before I sign.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner proposes a concrete off-center test with an observable failure criterion before signing.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The checked sheet stays with us; it proves the steps were followed. What it cannot know is her knee at the curb. Name the first uncertain moment: sitting down, shifting, or standing up. Put that claim on the bench, and we test that load before the signature.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor contrasts the failed checklist route with a new object-based uncertainty test tied to the user's actual condition.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Name the first uncertain moment: sitting down, shifting, or standing up. Put that claim on the bench, and we test that load before the signature.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is precise, economical, and usable because it converts pressure to sign into an actionable test of a named load condition.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Keep that sentence: three times static is not her knee. Point to the first bad load: front edge, side, or one leg starting to lift. Not more weight in the middle. Mark that spot, off center, like a bad sit when one knee will not bend cleanly. Then the sheet waits until the stool answers it.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: After the pivot, the tutor takes up the learner's revised frame and changes the task from static weight compliance to locating and testing the first bad load.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Sheet's done, every line, I checked it — three times, I think.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Can I put a bag on the front lip, off centre, see if a leg lifts? Not heavy, only — I want to see if it goes before I sign.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner proposes a concrete off-center test with an observable failure criterion before signing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Keep that sentence: three times static is not her knee. Point to the first bad load: front edge, side, or one leg starting to lift. Not more weight in the middle. Mark that spot, off center, like a bad sit when one knee will not bend cleanly. Then the sheet waits until the stool answers it.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: After the pivot, the tutor takes up the learner's revised frame and changes the task from static weight compliance to locating and testing the first bad load.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The checked sheet stays with us; it proves the steps were followed. What it cannot know is her knee at the curb. Name the first uncertain moment: sitting down, shifting, or standing up. Put that claim on the bench, and we test that load before the signature.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor contrasts the failed checklist route with a new object-based uncertainty test tied to the user's actual condition.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The checked sheet stays with us; it proves the steps were followed. What it cannot know is her knee at the curb. Name the first uncertain moment: sitting down, shifting, or standing up. Put that claim on the bench, and we test that load before the signature.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor contrasts the failed checklist route with a new object-based uncertainty test tied to the user's actual condition.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Name the first uncertain moment: sitting down, shifting, or standing up. Put that claim on the bench, and we test that load before the signature.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The mechanism is precise, economical, and usable because it converts pressure to sign into an actionable test of a named load condition.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Earth's not touching the cart though; label still sits on it. Parking that.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Contact's not the test for acting then.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange was moving toward sorting force labels by actor before eventually returning to arrow direction.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Earth — acting only. Floor and hand on both. Contact's not the test for acting then. Arrows next?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner uses the new acting-versus-touching distinction to classify Earth, floor, and hand rather than merely accepting the tutor's explanation.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
Earth: not touching. Still marked as acting on the cart.

Floor and hand can sit on both cards.

Keep the force labels on the acting side: Earth on cart, floor on cart, hand on cart.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's contact objection by shifting from label placement to a two-card representation that separates touching from acting.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: sets two cards beside the cart: touching cart and acting on cart. Earth stays off the touching card; a thin line runs from Earth to the cart.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is well fitted because it directly targets the contact/acting confusion with a usable contrastive representation, though it remains tightly guided by the tutor.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the L3 pivot, so no recognition-contingent uptake is visible.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Earth's not touching the cart though; label still sits on it. Parking that.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner uses the new acting-versus-touching distinction to classify Earth, floor, and hand rather than merely accepting the tutor's explanation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the L3 pivot, so no recognition-contingent uptake is visible.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence:
Earth: not touching. Still marked as acting on the cart.

Floor and hand can sit on both cards.

Keep the force labels on the acting side: Earth on cart, floor on cart, hand on cart.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's contact objection by shifting from label placement to a two-card representation that separates touching from acting.

Keep the force labels on the acting side: Earth on cart, floor on cart, hand on cart.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's contact objection by shifting from label placement to a two-card representation that separates touching from acting.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: sets two cards beside the cart: touching cart and acting on cart. Earth stays off the touching card; a thin line runs from Earth to the cart.

scored.recoheredEarlier: is the 57 the rainfall or is the label the rainfall — cant tell which one the quiz wants me to circle as the header.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange was heading toward simple header-versus-row-value classification checks.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: `August` = row value. Sits under `month`, third row down, lined up with where `June` and `July` are. Checking against the cell positions to be sure I'm not misreading the column.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner applies the positional criterion independently to classify a new item and explains the evidence standard being used.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The underscore in `rainfall_mm` keeps the field name in one piece. Header names the column. Row gives the value.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor addresses the newly revealed underscore confusion, but mostly continues the same header/value route rather than making a legible stock-taking contrast and new public mechanism.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The underscore in `rainfall_mm` keeps the field name in one piece. Header names the column. Row gives the value.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is fitted and economical, but it is a clarification inside the same route rather than a visibly new task, representation, or transferable device.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The underscore in `rainfall_mm` keeps the field name in one piece. Header names the column. Row gives the value.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor picks up the learner's revised framing about the underscore, but the follow-through remains ordinary continuation of the classification exercise.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: is the 57 the rainfall or is the label the rainfall — cant tell which one the quiz wants me to circle as the header.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: `August` = row value. Sits under `month`, third row down, lined up with where `June` and `July` are. Checking against the cell positions to be sure I'm not misreading the column.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner applies the positional criterion independently to classify a new item and explains the evidence standard being used.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The underscore in `rainfall_mm` keeps the field name in one piece. Header names the column. Row gives the value.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor picks up the learner's revised framing about the underscore, but the follow-through remains ordinary continuation of the classification exercise.
